findings.message: Destructive delete action with no confirmation step — a single misclick removes the item irreversibly, and there is no undo. Users who click Delete by accident have no way to recover.
findings.suggestion: Add a confirmation step before performing the DELETE (e.g. window.confirm or a confirmation dialog), or provide an undo affordance after deletion.

findings.message: No feedback while the DELETE request is in flight: the button is not disabled, there is no pending/loading indicator, and no error state if the request fails. Users may click repeatedly or see no response on slow networks.
findings.suggestion: Track a pending state and disable the button while the request is running; surface an error message (and re-enable retry) if the DELETE fails instead of silently calling window.location.reload().

findings.message: window.location.reload() on success is a heavy, jarring feedback mechanism — it discards UI state (scroll position, focus, any other in-flight work) and gives no explicit success indication that the item was deleted.
findings.suggestion: Update the items list in component state (e.g. lift state into Items and remove the deleted item from the array) and show a brief success indication (toast or inline message) instead of forcing a full page reload.
